story.iWant: to see what parameters are in my preset file before converting
story.soThat: I can verify it's the right file and understand what adjustments it contains
story.tasks:
## Tasks/Subtasks
1. **WASM Parameter Extraction Function** (AC-1) - [ ] 1.1: Add `extractParametersWrapper()` to cmd/wasm/main.go - [ ] 1.2: Implement `extractNP3Parameters()` using np3.ParseNP3 - [ ] 1.3: Implement `extractXMPParameters()` using xmp.ParseXMP - [ ] 1.4: Implement `extractLRTemplateParameters()` using lrtemplate.ParseLRTemplate - [ ] 1.5: Register `extractParameters` in WASM global scope - [ ] 1.6: Test all three formats return valid JSON
2. **Parameter Display Module** (AC-2, AC-3, AC-4) - [ ] 2.1: Create web/static/parameter-display.js - [ ] 2.2: Implement `displayParameters(fileData, format)` function - [ ] 2.3: Implement `renderParameterPanel()` with grouped display - [ ] 2.4: Implement `groupParameters()` to categorize by Basic/Color/Detail - [ ] 2.5: Implement `formatParameterValue()` with sign formatting - [ ] 2.6: Add CSS styles for parameter panel (web/static/style.css) - [ ] 2.7: Test parameter grouping for all three formats
3. **Collapsible Panel UI** (AC-5) - [ ] 3.1: Add toggle button to parameter header - [ ] 3.2: Implement `toggleParameterPanel()` function - [ ] 3.3: Persist collapse state during session - [ ] 3.4: Test collapse/expand behavior - [ ] 3.5: Test state persistence across file uploads
4. **Integration with Main Flow** (AC-1, AC-6, AC-7) - [ ] 4.1: Update main.js to import parameter-display.js - [ ] 4.2: Listen for 'formatDetected' event - [ ] 4.3: Call displayParameters() after format detection - [ ] 4.4: Add loading states (showParameterLoading/hideParameterLoading) - [ ] 4.5: Implement error handling with user-friendly messages - [ ] 4.6: Add HTML elements to index.html (parameterStatus, parameterError, parameterPanel) - [ ] 4.7: Test error handling with corrupted files - [ ] 4.8: Measure and verify <100ms extraction time
5. **Responsive Design & Polish** (AC-4) - [ ] 5.1: Test desktop layout (1920px) - [ ] 5.2: Test tablet layout (800px) - [ ] 5.3: Test mobile layout (400px) - single column stack - [ ] 5.4: Verify monospace font rendering for values - [ ] 5.5: Test dark mode compatibility (if applicable)
6. **Testing & Validation** (AC-7, DoD) - [ ] 6.1: Manual test with 10 NP3 files - [ ] 6.2: Manual test with 10 XMP files - [ ] 6.3: Manual test with 10 lrtemplate files - [ ] 6.4: Performance testing (20 files, log extraction times) - [ ] 6.5: Browser compatibility (Chrome, Firefox, Safari) - [ ] 6.6: Verify integration with Stories 2-2 and 2-3

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
### AC-1: Extract Parameters via WASM - [ ] Call new WASM function `extractParameters(fileData, format)` after format detection - [ ] Function returns JSON with parameter values - [ ] Handle all three formats: NP3, XMP, lrtemplate - [ ] Display loading state during extraction (<100ms expected)
### AC-2: Display Core Parameters (10-15 Key Settings) - [ ] Show parameters in organized sections: Basic, Color, Detail, Effects - [ ] Display format: "Parameter Name: Value" (e.g., "Exposure: +0.5") - [ ] Handle missing parameters gracefully (show "—" or omit)
### AC-3: Handle Format-Specific Parameters - [ ] NP3-specific: Sharpening (0-9), Mid-range Sharpening (0-7), Quick Adjust - [ ] XMP/lrtemplate-specific: Exposure2012, Contrast2012, Highlights2012, Shadows2012 - [ ] Show all parameters with original format names (no translation yet) - [ ] Group similar parameters
### AC-4: Styled Parameter Display Component - [ ] Layout: Two-column grid (parameter name | value) - [ ] Typography: Monospace font for values (alignment) - [ ] Colors: Subtle backgrounds for sections, bold for section headers - [ ] Responsiveness: Stack to single column on mobile (<768px)
### AC-5: Collapsible/Expandable Parameter View - [ ] Parameter panel initially expanded (show by default) - [ ] Click "Hide Parameters" → panel collapses - [ ] Click "Show Parameters" → panel expands - [ ] State persists during session
### AC-6: Error Handling for Parse Failures - [ ] Show message if extractParameters() fails: "Unable to extract parameters. File may be corrupted or unsupported." - [ ] Display format badge as "Unknown" on parse failure - [ ] Allow user to proceed with conversion anyway - [ ] Don't block conversion workflow
### AC-7: Performance Target - [ ] Parameter extraction completes <100ms (P95) - [ ] Display updates immediately (no perceptible lag) - [ ] No blocking of browser UI

doc.title: Technical Specification - Epic 2: Web Interface
doc.snippet: Shows extracted parameters before conversion. Uses WASM extractParameters() function. File structure: web/static/parameter-preview.js for display logic.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Display extracted parameters before conversion in tabular/list view. Shows 10-15 core parameters (Exposure, Contrast, etc.). Extraction completes <50ms. Builds trust and validates correct file.

doc.title: Story 2-2: File Upload Handling
doc.snippet: Provides file data as Uint8Array via getCurrentFileData(). File loaded using FileReader.readAsArrayBuffer(). Data accessible to format detection and conversion.

doc.title: Story 2-3: Format Detection
doc.snippet: Fires 'formatDetected' event with detected format ("np3", "xmp", "lrtemplate"). Uses WASM detectFormat() function. 100% accuracy on 1,479 test files.

artifact.reason: WASM bridge functions. Need to add extractParametersWrapper() following same Promise pattern. Registers Go functions as JavaScript global functions.
NP3 parser that returns UniversalRecipe. Will use this in extractNP3Parameters() to get parsed data, then convert to parameter map.
XMP parser with XML unmarshaling. Returns UniversalRecipe with Exposure2012, Contrast2012, etc. Will convert to JSON parameter map.
lrtemplate parser using regex extraction. Returns UniversalRecipe. Same Lightroom parameters as XMP (Exposure2012, etc.).
Main converter API. Thread-safe, stateless. Defines format constants (FormatNP3, FormatXMP, FormatLRTemplate). Reference for error handling patterns.
Provides Uint8Array file data. Story 2-4 will call getCurrentFileData() to get file data for parameter extraction.
WASM wrapper pattern reference. Shows how to call WASM functions, handle Promises, measure performance, and cache state. Follow this pattern for parameter-display.js.
Will integrate parameter display by listening to 'formatDetected' event and calling displayParameters().
Need to add: parameterStatus div, parameterError div, parameterPanel div for displaying parameters.
Will add parameter panel styles: .parameter-panel, .parameter-header, .parameter-grid, .parameter-row, responsive breakpoints.

constraints.constraint: Must use Epic 1 parsers (np3.Parse, xmp.Parse, lrtemplate.Parse). These are proven with 100% accuracy on 1,479 sample files. Do NOT create new parsing logic.
Parameter extraction must complete in <100ms (P95). Epic 1 parsers already meet this target (<30ms for XMP, <20ms for lrtemplate, <50ms for NP3).
All processing must be client-side. Zero network requests during conversion. WASM sandbox prevents malicious file attacks.
Follow existing JavaScript module pattern: export functions, handle async Promises, measure performance with performance.now(), use try/catch for error handling.
WASM functions must follow Promise pattern with resolve/reject handlers. Run operations in goroutines to avoid blocking. Use js.CopyBytesToGo for input, return JSON strings to JavaScript.
No external Lua libraries (lrtemplate uses regex). No external XML libraries (xmp uses encoding/xml). Keep dependencies minimal for WASM binary size.
Must work in Chrome, Firefox, Safari (latest versions). Use standard Web APIs only (FileReader, Promises, CustomEvents).
Manual testing with 10+ sample files per format. Measure extraction times. Verify responsive layout at 3 breakpoints (desktop, tablet, mobile).
Non-blocking errors. If parameter extraction fails, show error message but allow conversion to proceed. Parameters are informational, not required for conversion.
Follow existing UI patterns: status messages, loading states, error messages. Use same color scheme and typography as format detection badge.

interface.name: WASM Global Functions
interface.signature: convert(inputBytes: Uint8Array, fromFormat: string, toFormat: string): Promise<Uint8Array> detectFormat(inputBytes: Uint8Array): Promise<string> getVersion(): string extractParameters(inputBytes: Uint8Array, format: string): Promise<string> // NEW - to be added

interface.name: UniversalRecipe
interface.signature: type UniversalRecipe struct { Exposure, Contrast, Highlights, Shadows, Whites, Blacks float64; Vibrance, Saturation, Clarity, Sharpness float64; /* 50+ parameters */ }

interface.name: Parser Functions
interface.signature: np3.Parse(data []byte) (*models.UniversalRecipe, error) xmp.Parse(data []byte) (*models.UniversalRecipe, error) lrtemplate.Parse(data []byte) (*models.UniversalRecipe, error)

tests.standards: Manual browser testing is the primary testing approach for Epic 2 stories. Implementation quality verified through: 1) Code review against acceptance criteria, 2) Browser initialization testing (WASM loading, function availability), 3) Manual test execution with real sample files, 4) Performance measurement via console.time() and performance.now(), 5) Cross-browser compatibility verification (Chrome, Firefox, Safari - latest versions). Testing artifacts stored in story files with detailed test case documentation. Unit tests (Go) exist for Epic 1 parsers (internal/formats/*_test.go) achieving 100% accuracy on 1,479 sample files. Web UI testing focuses on integration and user experience validation.
tests.locations: - Go unit tests: internal/formats/np3/*_test.go, internal/formats/xmp/*_test.go, internal/formats/lrtemplate/*_test.go - Manual browser testing: Chrome DevTools, Firefox DevTools, Safari Web Inspector - Sample files: examples/np3/, examples/xmp/, examples/lrtemplate/ (1,479 test files from Epic 1) - Test documentation: Story markdown files contain manual testing checklists and test cases
tests.ideas:
AC-1: Extract Parameters via WASM - Upload Classic Chrome.np3 → verify extractParameters() called → returns JSON with ~15 parameters → completes <100ms - Upload .xmp file → verify Exposure2012, Contrast2012, etc. in returned JSON - Upload .lrtemplate file → verify same parameters as XMP (Lightroom format)
AC-2: Display Core Parameters - Upload NP3 → verify Basic section shows Exposure, Contrast, Highlights, Shadows, Whites, Blacks - Upload XMP → verify Color section shows Vibrance, Saturation, Temperature, Tint - Verify Detail section shows Clarity, Sharpness, Noise Reduction
AC-3: Handle Format-Specific Parameters - NP3 file → verify "Sharpening: 5", "Mid-range Sharpening: 3", "Quick Adjust" displayed - XMP file → verify "Exposure2012: +0.50", "Contrast2012: +20" displayed - Verify no confusion between NP3 and XMP parameter names
AC-4: Styled Parameter Display - Desktop (1920px) → verify two-column grid, monospace values, section backgrounds - Tablet (800px) → verify layout remains readable - Mobile (400px) → verify single-column stack, name/value stacked vertically
AC-5: Collapsible Panel - Upload file → panel expanded by default → click "Hide" → panel collapses - Upload another file → panel remains collapsed (respects preference) - Click "Show" → panel expands
AC-6: Error Handling - Create corrupted XMP (invalid XML) → upload → verify error message "Unable to extract parameters" - Verify conversion button still available (non-blocking error)
AC-7: Performance - Upload 10 NP3, 10 XMP, 10 lrtemplate files → measure extraction times → verify all <100ms (P95) - Verify browser remains responsive during extraction (no UI blocking)
